factors.summary: The company faces increased competition from technological developments and private label brands, which could negatively impact its business.

evidence.text: The markets in which we operate include a large number of participants [...] We may be unable to effectively compete on all these bases. [...] The continual development of new technologies, such as artificial intelligence and machine learning, by existing and new competitors, including non-traditional competitors with significant resources, could adversely affect our ability to sustain operating margins and desirable levels of sales volumes.
evidence.subsection: Increased competition, including from technological developments, could adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: The company's business is dependent on maintaining relationships with significant customers, which could be affected by changes in customer preferences or market conditions.

evidence.text: We have significant customers, particularly major retailers, although no one customer represented 10% or more of our total Net revenues in any of the past three fiscal years. [...] The loss or material reduction of business, either due to a reduction in demand from one or more of our significant customers [...] could have a material adverse impact on our business.
evidence.subsection: Changes in customer and consumer preferences and the inability to maintain beneficial relationships with large customers could adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: Global climate change and health crises pose risks to the company's operations and supply chain.

evidence.text: The effects of global climate change [...] could disrupt our operations by impacting the availability and cost of materials needed for manufacturing, cause physical damage or closure of our manufacturing sites or distribution centers, lead to loss of human capital and/or cause temporary or long-term disruption in the manufacturing or delivery of products and services to customers.
evidence.subsection: The effects of global climate change or other unexpected events, including global health crises, may disrupt our operations and have a negative impact on our business.

factors.summary: The company relies on IT systems and faces risks related to their performance and cybersecurity.

evidence.text: We rely extensively on information technology and operational technology systems, networks and services [...] There can be no assurance that our current IT Systems will function properly. [...] If our IT Systems cease to function properly [...] our ability to manage our operations could be impaired.
evidence.subsection: We may be subject to risks relating to our information technology and operational technology systems.

factors.summary: Inflation is negatively impacting the company's margins.

evidence.text: Elevated levels of inflation, including rising prices for raw materials, parts and components, freight, packaging, labor and energy, increases our costs to manufacture and distribute our products and services, and we may be unable to pass these increased costs on to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Increased prices and inflation could negatively impact our margin performance and our financial results.

factors.summary: Currency exchange rate fluctuations are affecting the company's financial results.

evidence.text: We are exposed to a variety of market risks, including the effects of changes in currency exchange rates. See 'Part II, Item 7A. Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations – Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk.' Approximately 25% of our 2023 Net revenues were derived outside the U.S., and we expect sales to non-U.S. customers to continue to represent a significant portion of our consolidated Net revenues. Although we may enter into currency exchange contracts to reduce our risk related to currency exchange fluctuations, changes in the relative fair values of currencies occur from time to time and in some instances have had a significant impact on our results of operations. We do not hedge against all our currency exposure, and therefore, our results of operations will continue to be susceptible to impacts from currency fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Currency exchange rate fluctuations have had, and may continue to have, an adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

factors.summary: Order backlog is stable as customers adjusted ordering patterns in response to reduced lead times.

evidence.text: As the year progressed, customers began adjusting ordering patterns in response to our reduced lead times due to improved supply chain and operational execution, which resulted in abnormal seasonality of non-residential revenues in 2023.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin electronic security products, which are outperforming mechanical products.

evidence.text: Growth in electronic security products and solutions remained strong throughout 2023 and continues to outperform mechanical products. We expect growth in the global electronic security product and solution categories we serve to continue to outperform growth in mechanical products and solutions over the long-term, as end-users adopt newer technologies in their facilities and homes.

factors.summary: Non-residential markets are stable with demand driven by improved pricing and supply chain execution, while residential markets are softening.

evidence.text: Net revenues from non-residential products (excluding Net revenues from our acquired Access Technologies business), increased by a low double-digits percent compared to the prior year, driven by improved pricing which was offset by lower volumes. Net revenues from residential products decreased by a low single digits percent compared to the prior year. Stable pricing was offset by lower volumes during the year.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is likely stable or improving given the improved supply chain and operational execution.

evidence.text: As the year progressed, customers began adjusting ordering patterns in response to our reduced lead times due to improved supply chain and operational execution, which resulted in abnormal seasonality of non-residential revenues in 2023.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are likely normalizing, supported by improved supply chain execution.

evidence.text: As the year progressed, customers began adjusting ordering patterns in response to our reduced lead times due to improved supply chain and operational execution, which resulted in abnormal seasonality of non-residential revenues in 2023.

evidence.text: We expect growth in the global electronic security product and solution categories we serve to continue to outperform growth in mechanical products and solutions over the long-term, as end-users adopt newer technologies in their facilities and homes. The security products industry will benefit from favorable long-term demographic trends such as continued urbanization of the global population, increased concerns about safety and security and technology-driven innovation.

evidence.text: The company sells a wide range of security products and solutions for end-users in commercial, institutional and residential facilities worldwide, including the education, healthcare, government, hospitality, retail, commercial office and single and multi-family residential markets. Our leading brands include CISA, Interflex, LCN, Schlage, SimonsVoss and Von Duprin.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates mixed evidence on pricing power. While it has successfully raised prices to offset lower volumes and mitigate inflation, there are concerns about its ability to fully offset cost increases, potentially impacting margins.

evidence.text: These decreases were partially offset by improved pricing across our major businesses throughout the segment as well as favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

Elevated levels of inflation, including rising prices for raw materials, parts and components, freight, packaging, labor and energy, increases our costs to manufacture and distribute our products and services, and we may be unable to pass these increased costs on to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Increased prices and inflation could negatively impact our margin performance and our financial results.

evidence.text: Increased pricing was the result of multiple pricing initiatives implemented to help mitigate the impact of inflation. We will continue to monitor the inflationary pressures to our businesses and address them through pricing initiatives where appropriate.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed across different regions and product lines. While volumes are declining in the Global Portable Security business and in residential products, they are stable or growing in non-residential products. Overall, volumes are negatively impacted by macroeconomic factors and supply chain issues.

evidence.text: The decrease in Net revenues was driven by lower volumes, particularly within our Global Portable Security business.
Ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers.

Net revenues from non-residential products (excluding Net revenues from our acquired Access Technologies business), increased by a low double-digits percent compared to the prior year, driven by improved pricing which was offset by lower volumes. Net revenues from residential products decreased by a low single digits percent compared to the prior year. Stable pricing was offset by lower volumes during the year.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with deceleration observed in certain regions and market segments, particularly in Global Portable Security and residential products, while non-residential demand remains stable. Macroeconomic conditions and market cyclicality are creating uncertainty, impacting institutional, commercial, and residential construction and remodeling markets, which could negatively affect demand and pricing.

evidence.text: A softening demand throughout much of Europe, Asia and Oceania in 2023 has impacted several of our businesses.

Demand for our security products and solutions relies on the institutional, commercial and residential construction and remodeling markets, which are marked by cyclicality based on overall economic conditions, including consumer confidence and disposable income, corporate and government spending, work-from-home trends, availability of credit and demand for new housing and infrastructure. Weakness or instability in one or more of these markets may cause current and potential customers to delay or cancel major capital projects or otherwise choose not to make purchases, which could negatively impact the demand for our products and solutions and erode average selling prices.
evidence.subsection: Our business relies on the institutional, commercial and residential construction and remodeling markets.

evidence.text: During 2023, we experienced stable demand for our non-residential products and services in our Allegion Americas segment. As the year progressed, customers began adjusting ordering patterns in response to our reduced lead times due to improved supply chain and operational execution, which resulted in abnormal seasonality of non-residential revenues in 2023. Macroeconomic conditions had a more challenging impact on the demand for our residential products in our Allegion Americas segment which negatively impacted revenues.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a deceleration in organic growth, driven by factors such as lower volumes, softening demand, and economic challenges. Despite efforts to maintain stable pricing, these issues have negatively impacted revenue across different regions and product segments.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the year ended December 31, 2023, decreased by 0.5%, or $4.0 million, as compared to the year ended December 31, 2022, due to the following: Pricing 4.9% Volume (7.4%) Acquisitions / divestitures 0.7% Currency exchange rates 1.3% Total (0.5%)

We must develop and commercialize new products and services that meet the varied and evolving needs of our customers and end-users in order to remain competitive in our current and future markets and in order to continue to grow our business.
evidence.subsection: Our growth is dependent, in part, on the development, commercialization and acceptance of new products and services.

evidence.text: Macroeconomic challenges, including ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages, cost inflation, rising interest rates and volatility in the capital markets, have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers.

Net revenues from residential products decreased by a low single digits percent compared to the prior year. Stable pricing was offset by lower volumes during the year. During 2023, we experienced a softening in market demand for our residential products, due in part to lower consumer sentiment, which resulted in reduced sales volumes.

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges with cost increases, particularly from inflation and restructuring activities, which are impacting margins. While they have implemented measures like price contracts and operational efficiencies to mitigate these costs, the effectiveness of these efforts is uncertain and may lead to further margin pressure.

evidence.text: Restructuring and integration expenses are increasing, impacting the margin trajectory.

To mitigate this exposure, we may use annual price contracts to minimize the impact of inflation and to benefit from deflation. Additionally, we are exposed to fluctuations in other costs such as packaging, freight, labor and energy prices. If inflation in these costs increases beyond our ability to control for them through measures such as implementing operating efficiencies, or we are not able to increase prices to sufficiently offset the effect of various cost increases without negatively impacting customer demand, our margin performance and results of operations would be negatively impacted.
evidence.subsection: Increased prices and inflation could negatively impact our margin performance and our financial results.

evidence.text: Cost of goods sold as a percentage of Net revenues decreased primarily due to the pricing and productivity improvements, which exceeded the impacts from inflation and investment spending, and lower restructuring and acquisition costs year-over-year.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed. While operating margins are under pressure due to unfavorable volume/product mix, increased restructuring expenses, and impairment charges, there are also positive factors such as expanding gross and operating margins driven by pricing and productivity improvements. Additionally, margins are facing headwinds from cost increases due to inflation, which may not be fully offset by price adjustments.

evidence.text: The decreases in Segment operating income and Segment operating margin were primarily driven by unfavorable volume/product mix, a year-over-year increase in restructuring, integration and acquisition expenses, and impairment charges on intangible assets recorded in the current year.

Elevated levels of inflation, including rising prices for raw materials, parts and components, freight, packaging, labor and energy, increases our costs to manufacture and distribute our products and services, and we do not currently use financial derivatives to hedge against volatility in commodity prices; however, we utilize firm purchase commitments, where possible, to help mitigate risk.
evidence.subsection: Increased prices and inflation could negatively impact our margin performance and our financial results.

evidence.text: Cost of goods sold as a percentage of Net revenues decreased to 56.7% from 59.6%, as compared to the year ended December 31, 2022, due to the following: Pricing and productivity in excess of inflation and investment spending (3.8) % Volume / product mix 0.3 % Acquisitions / divestitures 0.5 % Currency exchange rates 0.3 % Restructuring / integration / acquisition expenses (0.2) % Total (2.9) %

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving and stable, supporting both dividend payments and share repurchases.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities for the year ended December 31, 2023, increased by $141.1 million compared to 2022, driven primarily by higher net earnings and higher cash provided by working capital.

We paid quarterly dividends of $0.45 per ordinary share to shareholders on record as of March 15, 2023, June 15, 2023, September 18, 2023, and December 18, 2023, for a total of $158.7 million and repurchased approximately 0.5 million ordinary shares for approximately $59.9 million during the year ended December 31, 2023.

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges in retaining highly qualified personnel, which could hinder its growth.

evidence.text: Our ability to successfully grow and expand our business is dependent upon our ability to recruit and retain a workforce with the skills necessary to develop, manufacture and deliver the products and services desired by our customers.
evidence.subsection: Our ability to successfully grow and expand our business depends on our ability to recruit and retain a highly qualified and diverse workforce.

factors.summary: The company has increased its debt leverage due to new issuances and borrowings, while also showing a reduction in total borrowings outstanding and fixed-rate debt exposure. However, there is significant outstanding indebtedness with exposure to variable interest rates, which could lead to increased interest expenses.

evidence.text: Of our total outstanding indebtedness as of December 31, 2023, approximately 89% incurs fixed-rate interest and is therefore not exposed to the risk of rising variable interest rates.

We had approximately $2 billion of outstanding indebtedness at December 31, 2023. [...] The 2021 Credit Facilities had a combined outstanding variable rate balance of $225.0 million at December 31, 2023, which exposes us to variable interest rate risk. Applicable variable interest rates have increased throughout 2023, resulting in increased Interest expense.
evidence.subsection: There are risks associated with our outstanding and future indebtedness.

evidence.text: On June 22, 2022, Allegion US Holding Company Inc., a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company ('Allegion US Hold Co'), issued $600.0 million aggregate principal amount of its 5.411% Senior Notes due 2032 (the '5.411% Senior Notes'). The 5.411% Senior Notes require semi-annual interest payments on January 1 and July 1, and mature on July 1, 2032. We incurred and deferred $5.9 million of discounts and financing costs associated with the 5.411% Senior Notes, which is being amortized to Interest expense over their 10-year term, as well as $4.3 million of third party financing costs that were recorded within Interest expense on the Consolidated Statement of Comprehensive Income for the year ended December 31, 2022.

factors.summary: The company's supply chain has faced disruptions, including delays and shortages, which have negatively impacted its operations. However, there have been improvements in supply chain health, with reduced lead times and better operational execution observed in recent periods.

evidence.text: Disruptions in our global supply chain, including product manufacturing and logistical services provided by our supplier partners, may negatively impact our business.
evidence.subsection: Disruptions in our global supply chain, including product manufacturing and logistical services provided by our supplier partners, may negatively impact our business.

evidence.text: Ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers.

As the year progressed, customers began adjusting ordering patterns in response to our reduced lead times due to improved supply chain and operational execution, which resulted in abnormal seasonality of non-residential revenues in 2023.

factors.summary: The company is facing significant challenges due to unfavorable economic conditions, including macroeconomic headwinds such as supply chain disruptions, cost inflation, and potential recessions. While non-residential demand remains stable, residential products have been negatively impacted, and the company may face risks related to its ability to service debt in a challenging economic environment.

evidence.text: Ongoing macroeconomic challenges could adversely impact our business, results of operations, financial conditions and cash flows. Macroeconomic challenges, including ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages, cost inflation, rising interest rates and volatility in the capital markets, have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers.
evidence.subsection: There are risks associated with our outstanding and future indebtedness.

evidence.text: Deterioration of these macroeconomic conditions (or weakness in these conditions existing for an extended period of time), a decline in general economic activity or recession in the U.S. or global economy could slow demand for new construction or remodeling projects and result in our customers cancelling or delaying orders, which in turn could erode average selling prices and result in declines in our revenues, profitability and cash flows.

Macroeconomic conditions had a more challenging impact on the demand for our residential products in our Allegion Americas segment which negatively impacted revenues. We also experienced a continued softening of demand in our Global Portable Security and China businesses in our Allegion International segment.
evidence.subsection: Our business relies on the institutional, commercial and residential construction and remodeling markets.

factors.summary: Recent acquisitions have been successful in expanding product offerings and market reach, particularly with Access Technologies and plano.group.

evidence.text: The impact from the acquisition of plano in 2023 was partially offset by the divestiture of Milre in 2022.

On January 3, 2023, we acquired plano for a closing purchase price of $36.6 million. This acquisition was financed through cash on hand and borrowings under the 2021 Revolving Facility. Plano is a SaaS workforce management solution based in Germany, and has been incorporated into our Allegion International segment. On July 5, 2022, we completed the acquisition of the Access Technologies business for a purchase price of $915.2 million. This acquisition was financed by the net proceeds from the issuance of our 5.411% Senior Notes, together with borrowings under the 2021 Revolving Facility. The Access Technologies business has been integrated into our Allegion Americas segment. This acquisition helps us create a more comprehensive portfolio of access solutions, with the addition of automated entrance solutions. Additionally, the Access Technologies business adds an expansive service and support network throughout the U.S. and Canada, broadening our solutions to national, regional and local customers, and complementing our existing strengths in these non-residential markets.

factors.summary: The company faces multiple near-term risks, including economic downturns, supply chain disruptions, geopolitical tensions, softening demand in certain markets, and unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates. These factors could impact the business, operations, and financial health, potentially leading to material adverse effects.

evidence.text: Ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages, cost inflation, rising interest rates and volatility in the capital markets, have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers. These challenges may also make it more challenging for us to manufacture and deliver products to our customers, could cause periodic production interruptions and supply constraints, impact our ability to forecast and plan for future business activities and, if not adequately managed, could have a material adverse impact on our business, results of operations, financial condition and cash flows.

Macroeconomic conditions had a more challenging impact on the demand for our residential products in our Allegion Americas segment which negatively impacted revenues. We also experienced a continued softening of demand in our Global Portable Security and China businesses in our Allegion International segment. Unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements impacted several financial metrics.

factors.summary: The company is exposed to several key sensitivities, including macroeconomic conditions, foreign currency exchange rates, and competition in the electronic security product market. The company faces challenges from fluctuating currency exchange rates, which have negatively impacted its financial results, particularly in certain segments such as Allegion Americas and Allegion International. While the company does not hedge against all currency exposure, it acknowledges the significant impact of these fluctuations on its operations.

evidence.text: Approximately 25% of our 2023 Net revenues were derived outside the U.S., and we expect sales to non-U.S. customers to continue to represent a significant portion of our consolidated Net revenues. Although we may enter into currency exchange contracts to reduce our risk related to currency exchange fluctuations, changes in the relative fair values of currencies occur from time to time and in some instances have had a significant impact on our results of operations. We do not hedge against all our currency exposure, and therefore, our results of operations will continue to be susceptible to impacts from currency fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Currency exchange rate fluctuations have had, and may continue to have, an adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

evidence.text: Macroeconomic conditions had a more challenging impact on the demand for our residential products in our Allegion Americas segment which negatively impacted revenues. We also experienced a continued softening of demand in our Global Portable Security and China businesses in our Allegion International segment. Growth in electronic security products and solutions remained strong throughout 2023 and continues to outperform mechanical products.
